---

## ⚠️ Cap on Liability — Clauses Flagged for Legal Review

The following provisions were identified across multiple contracts. Each contains language that **limits recovery, caps damages, or restricts liability** upon breach — all of which should be carefully reviewed by a lawyer.

---

### 1. 📄 N2KINC Sponsorship Agreement (`N2KINC_10_16_1997-EX-10.16`)

> **"THE LIABILITY OF EXCITE FOR DAMAGES OR ALLEGED DAMAGES HEREUNDER, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR ANY OTHER LEGAL THEORY, IS LIMITED TO, AND WILL NOT EXCEED, THE AMOUNTS ACTUALLY PAID BY SPONSOR TO EXCITE HEREUNDER."**

**Why it needs review:**
- This is a **one-sided cap** — it limits only *Excite's* liability, not the Sponsor's.
- The cap is tied to amounts already paid, which may be **very low** relative to actual damages suffered.
- The cap applies broadly across **all legal theories** (contract, tort, etc.), which could severely restrict recovery options.

---

### 2. 📄 Pacira Pharmaceuticals Licensing Agreement (`PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING...`)

> **"Any and all liability of PPI to EKR howsoever arising... shall be limited (except for death or personal injury caused by the negligence of PPI or its employees...) to [**] US Dollars ($[**])..."**

> **"Any and all liability of EKR to PPI howsoever arising... shall be limited... to [**] US Dollars ($[**]); provided however that such limitation shall not apply to the extent that PPI or any PPI Indemnified Party is required to pay in excess of such amount to a third party in respect of a final judgment or order obtained by the third party."**

**Why it needs review:**
- The **actual dollar cap amounts are redacted** (`[**]`), meaning the specific financial limits are unknown and must be confirmed in the unredacted version.
- The cap applies to **both parties** but with **different carve-outs** — PPI's cap excludes death/personal injury, while EKR's cap has an additional carve-out for third-party judgments. This **asymmetry** warrants scrutiny.
- The phrase *"howsoever arising"* is extremely broad and could inadvertently waive significant claims.

---

### 3. 📄 Centrack International Web Site Hosting Agreement (`CENTRACKINTERNATIONALINC_10_29_1999-EX-10.3`)

> **"i-on's liability for damages to the Customer for any cause whatsoever, regardless of the form of action, and whether in contract or in tort, including negligence, shall be limited to one (1) month's fees and the remaining portion of any prepaid fees."**

> **"i-on will not be liable under any circumstances for any lost profits or other consequential damages, even if i-on has been advised as to the possibility of such damages."**

**Why it needs review:**
- The cap is **extremely low** — limited to just **one month's fees plus prepaid amounts** — which may be grossly inadequate for significant service failures.
- The exclusion of **lost profits and consequential damages** is a broad waiver that could leave the Customer with minimal recourse.
- This cap appears **entirely one-sided** in favor of i-on; no reciprocal cap on the Customer is mentioned.

---

### 4. 📄 Cyberian Outpost Promotion Agreement (`CYBERIANOUTPOSTINC_07_09_1998-EX-10.13`)

> **"NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), AND EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES."**

**Why it needs review:**
- This is a **mutual exclusion** of special, indirect, consequential, and incidental damages — a very broad waiver.
- The phrase *"however caused and on any theory of liability"* eliminates recovery even in cases of **negligence**, which may be overly aggressive.
- No **maximum dollar cap** is specified alongside this exclusion, which creates ambiguity about what damages *are* recoverable.

---

### 5. 📄 Harpoon Therapeutics Development Agreement (`HarpoonTherapeuticsInc_20200312_10-K_EX-10.18`)

> **"NEITHER PARTY NOR ANY OF ITS AFFILIATES SHALL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, PUNITIVE OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT, NEGLIGENCE, BREACH OF STATUTORY DUTY OR OTHERWISE..."**

**Why it needs review:**
- Excludes a **very wide range of damages** including punitive and exemplary damages, loss of profits, and business interruption.
- Carve-outs exist (referenced as `[***]` — redacted), meaning the **full scope of exceptions is unknown** without the unredacted document.
- The exclusion extends to **Affiliates**, which broadens the protection significantly.

---

## 🔑 Summary Table

| Contract | Cap Type | One-Sided? | Key Concern |
|---|---|---|---|
| N2KINC Sponsorship | Amounts paid to date | Yes (Excite only) | Very low cap; one-sided |
| Pacira Pharmaceuticals | Redacted dollar amount | No (both parties, asymmetric) | Redacted cap; asymmetric carve-outs |
| Centrack Hosting | 1 month's fees | Yes (i-on only) | Extremely low cap; no lost profits |
| Cyberian Outpost | No consequential damages | No (mutual) | Broad waiver; no dollar cap stated |
| Harpoon Therapeutics | No indirect/punitive damages | No (mutual) | Redacted carve-outs; very broad exclusion |

---

> ⚖️ **Recommendation:** All five provisions above should be reviewed by legal counsel, particularly to assess (1) whether caps are proportionate to potential exposure, (2) whether carve-outs are sufficient, and (3) whether any time limitations on bringing claims exist elsewhere in these agreements that were not captured in the retrieved sections.

---

## ⚖️ Non-Transferable License — Legal Review Highlights

---

### 🔴 1. **Dova Pharmaceuticals / Valeant — Promotion Agreement**
*(Source: DovaPharmaceuticalsInc_20181108_10-Q_EX-10.2)*

> *"[Rights] are **non-transferable, non-assignable, and non-delegable**. Except to Affiliates of Valeant, Valeant shall not subcontract the Valeant Activities with any Third Party... **Valeant shall not have any license rights hereunder nor any rights to sublicense any rights hereunder.**"*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Recommended:**
- The license is explicitly **non-transferable, non-assignable, and non-delegable** — this is a hard restriction.
- Valeant is **expressly prohibited from sublicensing** any rights, which is unusually restrictive.
- The **only exception** is performance through Valeant's own **Affiliates**, but even then, Valeant remains fully liable for their performance.
- A lawyer should assess whether this restriction adequately protects the client's operational flexibility, especially in scenarios involving corporate restructuring, M&A, or outsourcing needs.

---

### 🟡 2. **Harpoon Therapeutics / AbbVie — Development Agreement**
*(Source: HarpoonTherapeuticsInc_20200312_10-K_EX-10.18)*

> *"Without the prior written consent of the other Party, such **consent not to be unreasonably withheld**, conditioned, or delayed, neither Party shall sell, transfer, assign, delegate, pledge, or otherwise dispose of... this Agreement or any of its rights or duties hereunder; **provided that either Party may make such an assignment without the other Party's consent** to its Affiliate or to a successor, whether in a merger, sale of stock, sale of assets or any other transaction..."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Recommended:**
- Transfer to third parties requires **prior written consent**, though the standard of "not unreasonably withheld" provides some protection.
- There is a **carve-out** allowing assignment without consent to **Affiliates or successors** in M&A transactions — this is a key provision to evaluate.
- A lawyer should confirm whether the definition of "Affiliate" and "successor" is sufficiently broad to cover the client's corporate structure and future transactions.
- The clause that **void assignments** are "of no effect" should be reviewed to understand the consequences of an unauthorized transfer.

---

### 🟢 3. **Pacira Pharmaceuticals / EKR — Strategic Licensing Agreement**
*(Source: PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT)*

> *"Upon any termination or expiration of this Agreement, EKR shall promptly **transfer the Domain Names back to PPI**."*
> *"EKR shall receive **sole ownership** of, and good and valid title to, the Transferred NDA, free and clear of any liens and encumbrances."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Recommended:**
- While this agreement involves an **outright transfer of NDA ownership** (rather than a typical non-transferable license), the **reversion clause** for domain names upon termination has transfer implications.
- A lawyer should verify whether any license rights granted alongside the NDA transfer carry their own non-transferability restrictions not captured in the retrieved excerpts.

---

## 📋 Summary Table

| Dova / Valeant | **Strict — Non-transferable, non-assignable** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes (limited) | N/A |
| Harpoon / AbbVie | **Moderate — Consent required** | Not specified in excerpt | ✅ Yes (M&A too) | ✅ Yes (with reasonableness standard) |
| Pacira / EKR | Transfer of NDA ownership granted | Not specified in excerpt | Not specified | Not specified in excerpt |

Based on the retrieved knowledge base results, here is the analysis of **Third Party Beneficiary** clauses found across the relevant contracts:

---

## ⚖️ Third Party Beneficiary — Legal Review Analysis

### 🔴 Clauses Requiring Lawyer Review

---

#### 1. **Harpoon Therapeutics / AbbVie — Development Agreement**
> *"Except as provided in Article 11, covenants and agreements set forth in this Agreement are for the sole benefit of the Parties hereto and their successors and permitted assigns, and they shall not be construed as conferring any rights on any other Persons."*

**Why it needs review:**
- This clause **excludes** third-party beneficiary rights **except** for what is carved out in **Article 11** (which relates to indemnified parties).
- The carve-out for Article 11 means **certain non-contracting parties (indemnified parties) may still have enforceable rights** under the agreement.
- A lawyer should review **Article 11 in full** to determine the exact scope of who qualifies as an indemnified party and what rights they can enforce.

---

#### 2. **Hertz Global Holdings — Intellectual Property Agreement**
> *"With respect to indemnified parties, nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed to create any third-party beneficiary rights in any individual."*

**Why it needs review:**
- This clause **explicitly disclaims** third-party beneficiary rights, even for indemnified parties.
- However, the **scope of "any individual"** is notable — a lawyer should assess whether this language is broad enough to cover all potential claimants (e.g., corporate entities vs. individuals) and whether it is enforceable in the applicable jurisdiction.

---

#### 3. **Cherry Hill Mortgage — Strategic Alliance Agreement**
> *"Nothing in this Agreement is intended to confer upon any other Person any rights or remedies under or by reason of this Agreement."*

**Why it needs review:**
- This is a **broad exclusion** of third-party rights, but the use of "intended" (rather than "shall") could be interpreted as leaving room for **implied third-party rights** in some jurisdictions.
- A lawyer should confirm whether this language is sufficiently protective under the governing law of the agreement.

---

### 🟡 Clauses to Monitor (Lower Risk, But Worth Noting)

| Contract | Relevant Language | Concern |

---

## ⚠️ Anti-Assignment Clauses — Legal Review Recommended

### 1. 📄 Harpoon Therapeutics / AbbVie — Development Agreement
> *"Without the prior written consent of the other Party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed, neither Party shall sell, transfer, assign, delegate, pledge, or otherwise dispose of... this Agreement..."*

- ✅ **Consent required** from the other party for any assignment to a third party.
- ⚠️ **Exception:** Either party may assign **without consent** to an **Affiliate** or a **successor** (via merger, sale of stock, or sale of assets).
- 🔴 **Flag for lawyer:** The "not to be unreasonably withheld" standard is subjective and could be disputed. The scope of the affiliate/successor exception should be carefully reviewed.

---

### 2. 📄 Columbia Laboratories / Juniper Pharmaceuticals — Manufacturing and Supply Agreement (Amendment No. 2)
> *"Neither party shall, without the prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the other party having been obtained, assign or transfer this Agreement to any person or entity..."*

- ✅ **Consent required** for any third-party assignment.
- ⚠️ **Exception:** Assignment without consent is allowed to an **Affiliate** or **successor by merger**, or upon sale of all or substantially all assets — provided the acquirer confirms in writing its agreement to be bound.
- 🔴 **Flag for lawyer:** Notably, *"it shall not be deemed unreasonable for Columbia to withhold consent"* if it is not satisfied the assignee has adequate management, finances, personnel, capabilities, and facilities. This gives Columbia **broad discretion** to block Fleet's assignments — a significant asymmetry worth legal scrutiny.

---

### 3. 📄 Dova Pharmaceuticals / Valeant — Promotion Agreement
> *"...the other Party shall provide such Party written notice of its determination whether to provide such written consent within [***] following its receipt of such written notice..."*

- ✅ **Consent required**, with a **written notice process** for requesting and responding to consent.
- ⚠️ **Exception:** Either party may assign **without consent** to an **Affiliate**; Dova may also assign to a **successor in interest** in connection with a sale of substantially all assets related to the Product.
- 🔴 **Flag for lawyer:** The response timeframe for consent is **redacted ([***])**, meaning the specific deadline is unknown from this document. This gap should be clarified in the actual agreement.

---

### 4. 📄 Pacira Pharmaceuticals / EKR — Strategic Licensing, Distribution and Marketing Agreement
> *"Either Party may assign its rights hereunder to an Affiliate or to any successor by merger, consolidation, sale of stock or other equity interests or the sale of substantially all of the assets of such Party **without the consent** of the other Party."*

- ⚠️ **No consent required** for assignments to Affiliates or successors.
- 🔴 **Exception to the exception — Flag for lawyer:** *"Should there be a Change of Control of either Party resulting in the control of such Party by a Third Party which markets or sells a Competing Product... the rights under this Agreement may not be assigned **without the express consent** of the other Party."* This **competing product carve-out** is a critical restriction that warrants close legal review.

---

### 5. 📄 Hertz Global Holdings / HERC — Intellectual Property Agreement
> *"HERC may not assign, transfer, sublicense or delegate any of its rights hereunder... without the **prior written consent of HSI**, and any such purported assignment... shall be **void and without effect**."*

- ✅ **Strict consent required** — this is a **one-sided restriction** applying only to HERC, not HSI.
- 🔴 **Flag for lawyer:** This is notably **asymmetric** — only HERC needs consent; HSI appears to have no equivalent restriction. Any attempted assignment by HERC without consent is explicitly **void**. This imbalance should be reviewed carefully.

---

## 🔑 Summary Table

| Harpoon / AbbVie | ✅ Yes (mutual) | Not specified | Subjectivity of "unreasonably withheld" |
| Columbia / Juniper | ✅ Yes (mutual) | Not specified | Columbia's broad discretion to withhold |
| Dova / Valeant | ✅ Yes (mutual) | ✅ Yes (written) | Redacted response timeframe |
| Pacira / EKR | ❌ No (general) | ✅ Yes (sub-distributors) | Competing product change-of-control carve-out |
| Hertz / HERC | ✅ Yes (HERC only) | Not specified | One-sided restriction; void if violated |

### 📄 1. MSCI Inc. — Intellectual Property Agreement (`MSCIINC_02_28_2008-EX-10.10`)

**Relevant Provisions (Section 2 — Grant of License):**

> *"MS hereby grants (subject to any existing third party contractual obligations) to MSCI a **non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, world-wide, royalty-free** license for MSCI to **use, modify, copy, create derivative works of and sublicense**, for any business purpose, the MS Licensed Materials."*

#### 🔴 Flags for Legal Review:
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Broad sublicensing rights** | Both parties can sublicense "for any business purpose" — this is very broad and could expose IP to third parties without restriction. |
| **Perpetual & irrevocable** | The license cannot be terminated, even in the event of a breach. This is a significant risk. |
| **"Subject to existing third party contractual obligations"** | This carve-out is vague — it is unclear which third-party obligations could limit the license. |
| **No warranties ("AS-IS")** | Section 4 explicitly disclaims **all warranties**, including non-infringement. The licensee takes on full risk of IP infringement claims. |
| **No delivery obligation** | Section 3 states parties have "no obligation to provide copies of the Licensed Materials." The license may be practically unenforceable. |

---

### 📄 2. Arconic Rolled Products Corp. — Trademark License Agreement (`ArconicRolledProductsCorp_20191217_10-12B_EX-2.7`)

**Relevant Provisions (Section 1 — Grant and Scope of License):**

> *"Licensor on behalf of itself and its Affiliates hereby grants to Licensee the **limited licenses** to use and have used the Licensed Mark: (i) for the Licensed Products as set forth on Schedule 2; and (ii) as set forth on Schedule 1, concerning agreements entered into by Licensee prior to the Effective Date."*

#### 🔴 Flags for Legal Review:
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Sublicense extends to subsidiaries and joint venturers** | *"Such right to sublicense shall extend to Licensee's subsidiaries and joint venturers"* — scope of sublicensing should be carefully defined. |
| **Quality control & audit rights** | Licensor has broad rights to inspect premises, require samples, and terminate if quality standards are not met within **120 days** of notice. |
| **Termination risk** | Licensor can terminate if quality falls below standards and Licensee fails to remediate — this could be a significant business continuity risk. |
| **Trademark notices required** | All print and electronic displays must include ownership notices "at Licensor's option" — compliance burden on Licensee. |
| **Schedules 1 & 2 not fully reproduced** | The scope of the license depends on these schedules, which should be reviewed in full. |

---

### 📄 3. Harpoon Therapeutics / AbbVie — Development Agreement (`HarpoonTherapeuticsInc_20200312_10-K_EX-10.18`)

**Relevant Provisions:**

> *"Harpoon (on behalf of itself and its Affiliates) hereby grants to AbbVie [a license] under the Harpoon Patents, the Harpoon Know-How, and Harpoon's interests in the Joint Patents and the Joint Know-How, to **Exploit the Licensed Compounds and Licensed Products** in the Field in the Territory."*

| **Sublicense rights to Affiliates and third parties** | AbbVie can sublicense broadly, but *"AbbVie shall remain liable... for the performance of all Sublicensees"* — liability exposure should be assessed. |
| **Grants back to Harpoon** | AbbVie also grants back a license to Harpoon under AbbVie Patents — this cross-license structure requires careful IP ownership analysis. |

---

## ⚠️ Summary Recommendation

> **All three contracts contain license grants by one party to its counterparty and include provisions that should be reviewed by a lawyer**, particularly concerning:
> - **Scope and breadth** of rights granted (sublicensing, derivative works)
> - **Perpetual/irrevocable terms** with no termination rights

---

## ⚠️ Joint IP Ownership — Clauses Flagged for Legal Review

> **Source Document:** *HarpoonTherapeuticsInc_20200312_10-K_EX-10.18 — Development Agreement*

---

### 🔴 1. Joint Know-How & Joint Patents — Disclosure & Assignment Obligations
> *"Each Party will promptly disclose to the other Party in writing, the conception, discovery, development or making of any **Joint Know-How or Joint Patents** by Persons who perform activities for it under this Agreement. Each Party will execute and record assignments and other necessary documents consistent with such ownership promptly upon request."*

**Why review?** This clause creates a **mutual obligation to disclose and assign** jointly developed IP. The scope of what qualifies as "Joint Know-How" or "Joint Patents" is critical — if broadly interpreted, it could require assignment of IP that one party considers proprietary.

---

### 🔴 2. Prosecution & Maintenance of Joint Patents
> *"Harpoon shall have the right, but not the obligation, through the use of internal or outside counsel to prepare, file, prosecute, and maintain the **Harpoon Patents and Joint Patents** worldwide, at Harpoon's sole cost and expense."*

**Why review?** Harpoon bears the **sole cost** of prosecuting Joint Patents, yet both parties have interests in them. This creates an imbalance — a lawyer should assess whether cost-sharing provisions are adequate and whether AbbVie's rights are sufficiently protected.

---

### 🔴 3. AbbVie's Step-In Right for Joint Patents
> *"If Harpoon decides not to prepare, file, prosecute, or maintain a Harpoon Patent or Joint Patent in a country or other jurisdiction in the Territory, Harpoon shall provide reasonable prior written notice to AbbVie... AbbVie shall thereupon have the option, in its sole discretion, to assume the control and direction of the preparation, filing, prosecution, and maintenance of such Harpoon Patent or Joint Patent at its expense."*

**Why review?** AbbVie has a **step-in right** if Harpoon abandons prosecution of a Joint Patent. The notice timing is **redacted ([***])**, meaning a critical deadline is unknown from this excerpt. Legal review is essential to confirm the notice period is commercially reasonable and enforceable.

---

### 🔴 4. Defense & Control of Joint Patents
> *"The Party who is prosecuting the Joint Patents at the relevant time shall have the **first right**, but not the obligation, to defend and control the defense of the validity and enforceability of the Joint Patents... The other Party may participate... with counsel of its choice at its own expense."*

**Why review?** Control of defense of Joint Patents shifts depending on **who is currently prosecuting** them — this is a dynamic and potentially ambiguous arrangement. A lawyer should assess whether this creates gaps in protection or conflicts of interest.

---

### 🔴 5. Governing Law for IP Ownership Determination
> *"The determination of whether Information and inventions are conceived, discovered, developed, or otherwise made by a Party for the purpose of allocating proprietary rights (including Patent, copyright or other intellectual property rights) therein, shall, for purposes of this Agreement, be made in accordance with **Applicable Law in the United States**."*

**Why review?** This clause anchors joint ownership determinations to **U.S. law**, which has specific and sometimes unfavorable rules for joint IP ownership (e.g., each co-owner can independently exploit jointly owned patents without consent or accounting to the other). Legal counsel should evaluate the implications of this choice of law.

---

### 🟡 6. No Joint IP in the Strategic Alliance Agreement
> *"Except as expressly agreed otherwise in writing, all Intellectual Property Rights vested in a Party prior to the date of this Agreement shall remain vested in that Party. Further, any improvements, enhancements, modifications or developments to a Party's intellectual property shall **automatically vest in that Party** irrespective of who generates the relevant improvement, enhancement, modification or development."*
> *(Source: WASTE2ENERGYHOLDINGS, INC — Strategic Alliance Agreement)*

**Note:** This second contract **explicitly avoids joint ownership** — improvements vest solely in the originating party. This is a notably different approach and may be worth flagging as a **comparison point** for how joint IP risk can be mitigated.

---

|---|---|---|
| Joint Know-How/Patent Disclosure & Assignment | 🔴 High | Broad scope of joint IP obligations |
| Harpoon's Prosecution of Joint Patents | 🔴 High | Cost imbalance; sole control by one party |
| AbbVie's Step-In Right (redacted notice period) | 🔴 High | Critical deadline is redacted/unknown |
| Defense & Control of Joint Patents | 🔴 High | Ambiguous shifting control mechanism |

## ⚠️ Uncapped Liability — Clauses Requiring Legal Review

The knowledge base returned relevant provisions from **five contracts**. Below are the flagged clauses, organized by contract and risk type.

### 1. 🔴 PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS — Strategic Licensing, Distribution and Marketing Agreement

**Section 9 – EKR Liability Limitation**

> *"Any and all liability of EKR to PPI howsoever arising... shall be limited... to [**] US Dollars ($[**]); **provided however that such limitation shall not apply** to the extent that PPI or any PPI Indemnified Party is required to pay in excess of such amount to a third party in respect of a **final judgment or order obtained by the third party**."*

**⚠️ Why this needs legal review:**
- The liability cap is **explicitly lifted** when a third-party final judgment or court order exceeds the capped amount. This creates a scenario where EKR's liability becomes **effectively uncapped** in third-party litigation, as it tracks whatever a court awards — with no ceiling stated.
- The cap also does **not apply** to death or personal injury caused by EKR's negligence, or to unpaid specified payments, milestones, or royalties.

---

### 2. 🔴 WASTE2ENERGY HOLDINGS — Strategic Alliance Agreement

**Clause 10.4 – IP Indemnification (Uncapped)**

> *"Each Party shall **indemnify and keep indemnified** the other Party against **all costs, claims, losses, expenses and damages** incurred by the other Party as a result of any breach of the provisions set out in this Clause 10 or arising directly or indirectly out of any **infringement by that Party of the other Party's Intellectual Property Rights**."*

**⚠️ Why this needs legal review:**
- The IP infringement indemnity covers **"all costs, claims, losses, expenses and damages"** with **no stated cap or ceiling**. This is a classic uncapped liability exposure for IP breach.

**Clause 11.10 – Confidentiality Breach (Injunctive Relief + Uncapped Remedies)**

> *"Each Party acknowledges that **damages alone would not be an adequate remedy** in the event of breach... Accordingly, it is agreed that either Party shall be entitled, **without proof of special damages**, to seek an **injunction or other interim remedy** for any threatened or actual breach of this Clause, **without prejudice to any other rights and remedies** which that Party may have."*

**⚠️ Why this needs legal review:**
- Breach of confidentiality entitles the aggrieved party to injunctive relief **plus** all other legal remedies, with no cap on damages. The phrase "without prejudice to any other rights and remedies" means monetary liability remains fully open-ended.

---

### 3. 🟡 N2K INC. — Sponsorship Agreement (Excite)

**Section 15 – Dispute Resolution: Trademarks & Confidentiality Carve-Out**

> *"The parties agree that any breach of either of the parties' obligations regarding **trademarks, service marks or trade names and/or confidentiality** would result in **irreparable injury** for which there is no adequate remedy at law. Therefore... the aggrieved party will be entitled to seek **equitable relief in addition to its other available legal remedies**."*

**⚠️ Why this needs legal review:**
- Breaches of **trademark/IP and confidentiality obligations are carved out** from the standard arbitration/mediation process and are subject to court proceedings with equitable relief **plus** all other legal remedies — effectively uncapped.
- Note: Excite's general liability *is* capped to "amounts actually paid by Sponsor to Excite," but this carve-out for IP/confidentiality breaches **bypasses that cap**.

---

### 4. 🟡 DOVA PHARMACEUTICALS — Promotion Agreement

**Section 4 – Limitation of Liability: Confidentiality Carve-Out**

> *"IN NO EVENT SHALL DOVA... OR VALEANT... BE LIABLE... FOR ANY CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, PUNITIVE OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES... **except**... (2) **damages available for a Party's breach of the confidentiality and non-use obligations in Article 9**."*

**⚠️ Why this needs legal review:**
- The exclusion of consequential/punitive damages is **explicitly carved out** for **confidentiality and non-use obligation breaches**. This means a party breaching confidentiality could face **uncapped consequential and punitive damages** — a significant financial exposure.

---

### 5. 🟢 WEBHELP.COM / eGAIN — Hosting Agreement

**Section 9 – IP Infringement Indemnification**

> *"eGain will defend any third-party suit... based on a claim that the Software or the eGain System **infringes any valid United States patent, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary right**, and eGain will **pay those damages and costs finally awarded** against Customer in any monetary settlement."*
> *"THE FOREGOING STATES eGAIN'S ENTIRE LIABILITY FOR INFRINGEMENT CLAIMS."*

**⚠️ Why this needs legal review:**
- eGain's IP infringement liability is tied to **"damages and costs finally awarded"** by a court or settlement — with no stated dollar cap. While the clause states this is eGain's "entire liability," the amount itself is uncapped and determined by third-party proceedings.

Based on the retrieved knowledge base documents, here is a structured analysis of **Change of Control** provisions found across the contracts, highlighting the clauses that warrant legal review.

---

## ⚠️ Change of Control — Legal Review Highlights

---

### 1. 📄 Pacira Pharmaceuticals — Strategic Licensing, Distribution and Marketing Agreement

#### 🔍 Definition of Change of Control
The contract defines "Change of Control" as:
> *"(a) the sale, lease, exchange, license or disposition of all or substantially all of the Party's assets in one transaction or series of related transactions or (b) a merger or consolidation with an unaffiliated Third Party as a result of which the holders of the Party's issued and outstanding voting securities immediately before such transaction own or control less than a majority of the voting securities of the continuing or surviving entity immediately after such transaction."*

It further **expands** the definition specifically for PPI to include:
> *"any sale of securities of PPI or its Affiliates directly by the holder... in which such sale results in a transfer of more than 50% of the outstanding voting stock of PPI or its Affiliates."*

> ⚠️ **Flag for Legal Review:** The definition is **asymmetric** — the expanded definition (including stock sales >50%) applies to PPI and its Affiliates but not necessarily to the counterparty (EKR). A lawyer should confirm whether this asymmetry is intentional and acceptable.

---

#### 🔍 Assignment & Change of Control Consent Requirement
> *"Should there be a Change of Control of either Party resulting in the control of such Party by a Third Party which markets or sells a Competing Product in any part of the Territory, then the rights under this Agreement may not be assigned without the express consent of the other Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld."*

> ⚠️ **Flag for Legal Review:**
> - Consent is **required** only when the acquirer sells a **Competing Product**. This is a narrow but critical trigger — a lawyer should assess how "Competing Product" is defined and whether this adequately protects both parties.
> - The standard is "not unreasonably withheld," but **no termination right** is explicitly granted — only a consent right on assignment. A lawyer should confirm whether this is sufficient protection or whether a termination right should also be negotiated.

---

#### 🔍 Assignment Without Consent (General Rule)
> *"Either Party may assign its rights hereunder to an Affiliate or to any successor by merger, consolidation, sale of stock or other equity interests or the sale of substantially all of the assets of such Party **without the consent of the other Party**."*

> ⚠️ **Flag for Legal Review:** This broad carve-out allows assignment in a merger, stock sale, or asset sale **without any notice or consent**, except in the Competing Product scenario above. A lawyer should evaluate whether the counterparty should at minimum receive **notice** of such an assignment.

---

### 2. 📄 Harpoon Therapeutics / AbbVie — Development Agreement

#### 🔍 Assignment Clause
> *"Without the prior written consent of the other Party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed, neither Party shall sell, transfer, assign, delegate, pledge, or otherwise dispose of, whether voluntarily, involuntarily, **by operation of law or otherwise**, this Agreement or any of its rights or duties hereunder; **provided that** either Party may make such an assignment without the other Party's consent to its Affiliate or to a successor, whether in a merger, sale of stock, sale of assets or any other transaction, of the business to which this Agreement relates."*

> ⚠️ **Flag for Legal Review:**
> - The general rule requires **prior written consent** (not unreasonably withheld) — this is protective.
> - However, the **carve-out for successors** (merger, stock sale, asset sale) is broad and **removes the consent requirement** entirely for those scenarios.
> - The phrase **"by operation of law"** is explicitly covered, which is notable — a lawyer should confirm this aligns with the parties' intent, particularly in insolvency or statutory merger scenarios.
> - A **redacted section** (`[***]`) exists in Section 4.2, which may contain additional Change of Control-specific rights or restrictions. **This redacted content must be reviewed by a lawyer.**

#### 🔍 Change of Control Definition (Harpoon Agreement)
The agreement defines a Change of Control event to include when:
> *"such Party enters into a merger, consolidation or similar transaction... and as a result... (a) the members of the Board of Directors... immediately prior to such transaction constitute less than a majority... immediately following such transaction or (b) the Persons that beneficially owned... shares of Voting Stock... cease to beneficially own... shares of Voting Stock... representing at least a majority of the total voting power..."*

Also includes:
> *"such Party sells or transfers to any Third Party... properties or assets representing all or substantially all of such Party's assets to which this Agreement relates"* and *"the holders of capital stock of such Party approve a plan or proposal for the **liquidation or dissolution** of such Party."*

> ⚠️ **Flag for Legal Review:** The inclusion of **liquidation or dissolution** as a Change of Control trigger is significant and may have implications for wind-down scenarios. A lawyer should assess the downstream consequences (e.g., license termination, reversion of rights) triggered by this definition.

---

### 3. 📄 Columbia Laboratories / Juniper Pharmaceuticals — Manufacturing and Supply Agreement (Amendment No. 2)

#### 🔍 Assignment Clause
> *"Neither party shall, without the prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the other party... assign or transfer this Agreement... provided that, each party may assign or transfer this Agreement **without such consent** to any Affiliate or to any successor by merger of such party, or upon a sale or other transfer of all or substantially all of such party's assets or business..."*

> ⚠️ **Flag for Legal Review:**
> - The carve-out again removes consent for mergers and asset sales, but adds a condition: *"the acquirer of the business confirms to the Supplier in writing its agreement to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement."* A lawyer should verify this written confirmation requirement is enforceable and whether there is a **deadline** for providing it.
> - Notably, the agreement adds: *"it shall not be deemed unreasonable for Columbia to withhold consent, to any proposed or attempted assignment (including by merger or sale) by Fleet to a party which is not an Affiliate, if Columbia is not reasonably satisfied that the assignee possesses the management, finances, personnel, capabilities and facilities to perform fully the obligations of Fleet hereunder."* This gives Columbia **significant practical veto power** over Fleet's assignments. A lawyer should assess whether this is balanced and whether "reasonably satisfied" is sufficiently defined.

---

## 🔑 Summary Table

| Contract | Consent Required? | Notice Required? | Termination Right? | Key Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Pacira / EKR** | Only if acquirer sells Competing Product | ❌ Not specified | ❌ Not explicit | Asymmetric CoC definition; no termination right |
| **Harpoon / AbbVie** | Yes (general), but **waived** for successors | ❌ Not specified | Redacted `[***]` — unknown | Broad successor carve-out; redacted terms |
| **Columbia / Fleet** | Yes (general), but **waived** for mergers/asset sales | ❌ Not specified | ❌ Not explicit | Columbia's broad veto power over Fleet |

---

> **⚠️ Overall Recommendation:** A lawyer should review all three contracts, with particular attention to: (1) the **absence of explicit notice requirements** upon a Change of Control in all agreements; (2) the **broad successor carve-outs** that eliminate consent requirements; (3) the **redacted provisions** in the Harpoon/AbbVie agreement; and (4) the **asymmetric CoC definition** in the Pacira agreement.

- **Risk:** PPI has unilateral discretion to cut off post-termination sales at any time within the 3-month window. This asymmetry in control favors PPI and should be scrutinized.

**C. Transfer of Manufacturing & IP to EKR**

PPI shall provide EKR with access to all relevant PPI Know-How, and any other information necessary for EKR to transfer such manufacturing to an alternate manufacturer."*

## ⚠️ Anti-Assignment Clauses — Legal Review Highlights

The following contract excerpts contain Anti-Assignment language that **should be reviewed by a lawyer**, particularly around the conditions under which consent or notice is required.

---

### 1. 🔴 Harpoon Therapeutics / AbbVie — Development Agreement
**Source:** *HarpoonTherapeuticsInc_20200312_10-K_EX-10.18 — Development Agreement*

> *"Without the prior written consent of the other Party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed, neither Party shall sell, transfer, assign, delegate, pledge, or otherwise dispose of, whether voluntarily, involuntarily, by operation of law or otherwise, this Agreement or any of its rights or duties hereunder; provided that either Party may make such an assignment without the other Party's consent to its Affiliate or to a successor, whether in a merger, sale of stock, sale of assets or any other transaction..."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Points:**
- **Consent IS required** for assignment to a third party (prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld).
- **Consent is NOT required** for assignment to an Affiliate or successor in a merger/sale — a broad carve-out that could allow significant changes in control without consent.
- The assigning party remains responsible for Affiliate performance — scope of this liability should be reviewed.

---

### 2. 🔴 Columbia Laboratories / Fleet — Manufacturing and Supply Agreement (Amendment No. 2)
**Source:** *Columbia Laboratories, (Bermuda) Ltd. — AMEND NO. 2 TO MANUFACTURING AND SUPPLY AGREEMENT*

> *"Neither party shall, without the prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the other party having been obtained, assign or transfer this Agreement to any person or entity, in whole or in part (and any attempt to do so shall be void)..."*

> *"Notwithstanding the foregoing, it shall not be deemed unreasonable for Columbia to withhold consent, to any proposed or attempted assignment (including by merger or sale) by Fleet to a party which is not an Affiliate, if Columbia is not reasonably satisfied that the assignee possesses the management, finances, personnel, capabilities and facilities to perform fully the obligations of Fleet hereunder."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Points:**
- **Consent IS required** for any third-party assignment (prior written consent).
- Columbia has an **explicit right to withhold consent** if the proposed assignee lacks sufficient management, finances, personnel, capabilities, or facilities — this is a notably **subjective and broad standard** that warrants scrutiny.
- The acquirer must **confirm in writing** their agreement to be bound by all terms — a procedural requirement that must be carefully managed.

---

### 3. 🔴 Dova Pharmaceuticals / Valeant — Promotion Agreement
**Source:** *DovaPharmaceuticalsInc_20181108_10-Q_EX-10.2 — Promotion Agreement*

> *"The other Party shall provide such Party written notice of its determination whether to provide such written consent within [***] following its receipt of such written notice..."*

> *"(a) either Party may, without the other Party's consent, assign this Agreement and its rights and obligations hereunder in whole or in part to an Affiliate; and (b) Dova may assign this Agreement to a successor in interest in connection with the sale or other transfer of all or substantially all of Dova's assets or rights relating to the Product..."*

> *"[Rights] are non-transferable, non-assignable, and non-delegable. Except to Affiliates of Valeant, Valeant shall not subcontract the Valeant Activities with any Third Party..."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Points:**
- **Consent IS required** for third-party assignment, with a written response required within a **redacted timeframe (`[***]`)** — this specific deadline is not visible and should be confirmed in the original document.
- **Valeant's rights are explicitly non-transferable, non-assignable, and non-delegable** — a strict restriction that could have significant operational implications.
- Dova has a **unilateral right** to assign to a successor upon sale of substantially all assets, without Valeant's consent — an asymmetric provision favoring Dova.

---

### 4. 🔴 PACIRA Pharmaceuticals / EKR — Strategic Licensing, Distribution and Marketing Agreement
**Source:** *PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. — A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT*

> *"Either Party may assign its rights hereunder to an Affiliate or to any successor by merger, consolidation, sale of stock or other equity interests or the sale of substantially all of the assets of such Party without the consent of the other Party."*

> *"Should there be a Change of Control of either Party resulting in the control of such Party by a Third Party which markets or sells a Competing Product in any part of the Territory, then the rights under this Agreement may not be assigned without the express consent of the other Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Points:**
- **No consent required** for assignment to Affiliates or successors in standard M&A transactions — a broad carve-out.
- **Express consent IS required** in a Change of Control scenario where the acquiring third party sells a **Competing Product** — a critical competitive protection clause that needs careful definition of what constitutes a "Competing Product."

### 5. 🔴 Hertz Global Holdings / HERC — Intellectual Property Agreement
**Source:** *HERTZGLOBALHOLDINGS,INC_07_07_2016-EX-10.4 — Intellectual Property Agreement*

> *"HERC may not assign, transfer, sublicense or delegate any of its rights hereunder or delegate its obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of HSI, and any such purported assignment, transfer, sublicense or delegation, in the absence of such consent, shall be void and without effect."*

**⚠️ Lawyer Review Points:**
- **Consent IS required** — and this is a **one-sided restriction** applying only to HERC, not HSI. This asymmetry is significant and should be reviewed.
- Any unauthorized assignment is explicitly **void and without effect** — a strong enforcement mechanism.
- No Affiliate or successor carve-out is mentioned for HERC in the retrieved text — if none exists, this is a notably strict restriction.

## 📋 Audit Rights — Summary & Legal Review Highlights

### 1. **Dova's Right to Audit Valeant** *(Dova Pharmaceuticals / Valeant Promotion Agreement)*

| Frequency limited to **once per every 12 months** during the Term, and **once during the one (1) year period** following the end of the Term | This restriction may be insufficient if ongoing compliance issues arise. |

| "Undisputed adjustments" language | What happens to *disputed* adjustments is not addressed in the retrieved text — a lawyer should confirm whether a dispute resolution mechanism exists. |

### 3. **Columbia's Right to Audit Fleet's Manufacturing Facility** *(Columbia Laboratories / Fleet Manufacturing and Supply Agreement)*

**What it says:**
